question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight different spices viz. Coriander, Fennel,

Jeera, Basil, Pepper, Cinnamon, Oregano, and

Turmeric are placed in a square box such that

all of them are facing towards the center, but

not necessarily in the same order. Four items are

placed at the corners while four items are

placed at the middle of the sides.

Cinnamon is placed at one of the corners of the

box. Two items are placed between Cinnamon

and Pepper, which is placed to the immediate

left of Oregano. The number of items between

Oregano and Cinnamon is the same as the

number 
of
items 
between 
Pepper 
and

Coriander when counted from the left of

Cinnamon and Pepper. One item is placed

between Coriander and Turmeric, which is not

placed adjacent to
Oregano. Fennel was

placed to the immediate left of Jeera.

How many items are placed between Jeera

and Oregano when counted from the left of

Jeera?

According to the common solution we can say

Hence, the correct answer is option C.

given below.

around a square table facing towards the

center of the table. Four persons are sitting

center the sides of the table while four are

sitting at the corner of the table. The one who

are sitting in the center the sides of the table

like
different actresses
i.e. Alia,
Deepika,

Kareena and Kiara and the one who are sitting

at the corner of the square table like different

actor i.e. Akshay, Salman, Ranveer and Kartik.

I sits third to the left of the one who likes Kartik.

M likes Kiara and sits to the immediate right of

N, who does not like Kartik. O sits opposite to

the one who likes Alia. J sits to the immediate

right of L. The one who likes Ranveer sits to the

immediate left of the one who likes Deepika. O

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight persons -P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W are

sitting on the square table such that all of them

are
facing towards the centre, but not

necessarily in the same order. Four of them sits

at the corner and four are sitting at the sides. S

sits second to the left of R who sits immediate

left of T. S sits second to the right of Q. When

counted from the right of Q, the number of

persons sit between Q and P is the same as the

number of persons sit between W and P. P sits

to the immediate right of S. One person sit

between V and U, who do not sit in the middle

of the side. U is an immediate neighbour of S.

Who among the following person sits second to

the left of V?

question:
Directions: Study the following information

carefully and answer the given questions.

Twelve students- A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K and

L sit around the square table for the chemistry

practical, such that three students sit on each

middle side of a table. Out of the three students

on each side, the middle one is facing outwards

while the other two students are facing inwards.

The students facing inwards have different

metals viz., Mg, Br, Fe, Li, Ga, Tc, Cs and Er. The

distance between the two students is equal.

Note: Y is a neighbour of X and Z, or Y sits

adjacent to X and Z, means Y sits between X

and Z on the same side.

D is neighbour of the students who have 'Cs'

and 'Tc'. L faces the person who is second to

the right of F. E sits third to the right of A. One

student sits between A and L while five students

sit between A and D. I faces outwards and sits

fourth to the right of K. A is adjacent to students

who have 'Mg' and 'Fe'. H faces J and both

have neither 'Mg' nor 'Tc'. G sits second to the

right of J. The students having 'Er' and 'Br' are

opposite to each other. C has 'Li' and sits

second to the right of one who has 'Br'. E is a

neighbour of B and F.

K faces the student who has metal ____ in the

chemistry practical.

question:
Directions : Study the following information

carefully and answer
the questions given

below.

Eight persons- J, K, L, M, N, O, P and Q are

sitting around a square table in such a way that

one person sits in the middle of each side and

one person sits on each corner of the table.

Some of them are facing towards the center

while some others are facing away from the

center of the table. At least three persons

sitting at the corners facing away from the

center. Q sits second to the left of O who does

not sit in the middle of the sides. Only two

persons sit between K and O when counted

questionHtml:
Directions : Study the following information

carefully and answer
the questions given

below.

Eight persons- J, K, L, M, N, O, P and Q are

sitting around a square table in such a way that

one person sits in the middle of each side and

one person sits on each corner of the table.

Some of them are facing towards the center

while some others are facing away from the

center of the table. At least three persons

sitting at the corners facing away from the

center. Q sits second to the left of O who does

not sit in the middle of the sides. Only two

persons sit between K and O when counted
explanation:
We have, Q sits second to the left of O who

does not sit in the middle of the sides. Only two

persons sit between K and O when counted

from the left of O. From the above conditions,

there are two possibilities.

Again we have, N sits immediate left of K. One

person sits between N and J. Immediate

neighbors of N do not face the same direction.

Again we have, L faces the centre and sits

second to the right of P. M does not sit opposite

to L. The one who sits opposite to M does not

face the centre. Both J and Q face opposite

directions to each other. Immediate neighbors

of P face the same direction as P From the

above conditions, case-2 gets eliminated.

Hence, case-1 shows the final arrangement.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H are the eight girls

seated on a square table, four of whom are in

the corners facing the outside and four of

whom are in the middle of the sides facing the

inside of the table.

Between A and E, only three girls are seated.

Third from the left of D is E. When counting from

the right of both A and E, as many girls are

seated between D and A as between E and H.

The girl who is second from H's left is sitting

opposite from B. F is sitting adjacent to the girl

seated second from G's left. Between G and A,

at least one girl is seated. C does not face

away from the table.

The next four of the next five are similar in one

particular manner and thus make a group. Find

the one who is not a part of that group.

1. On the same square, Gayatri and Himanshu

2. Binod, who is seated immediately to the right

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight Persons viz, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are

sitting on a square table and all of them are

faces inside but not necessarily in the same

order. Four of them sit on the corner of the table

and the rest of them sit on the side of the table.

Further, it is known that all persons have either

different or the same weights.

W sits third to the right of Q. S’s weight is more

than at most five persons but he’s not the

lightest among all. Q’s weight is more than W's.

The number of persons sitting between V and

W is the same as between R and S. Only two

persons sit between W and the one who is

immediate right of R, who is not adjacent to Q.

V’s weight is more than S and T. Only three

persons' weights are between W and R. V’s

weight is more than W’s and sits immediate

right of Q. U’s weight is the second lowest

among them. The one whose weight is lower

than S but more than at least two persons is T.

Both W and R have lower weights than S. T sits

third to the left of V, who is not sitting on the

side of the table. P’s weight equals U’s, who sits

opposite Q.

Which of the following is definitely false?

Case1a > > > > > > S >

Case2a > > > > > S > >

Case3a > > > > S > > >

Case4a > > > S > > > >

Case5a > > S > > > > >

Q’s weight is more than W’s. V’s weight is more

than S and T. The number of persons sitting

between V and W is the same as between R

and S. V’s weight is more than W’s and sits

immediate right of Q. U’s weight is the second

lowest among them. From this condition, Case

1a and Case 5a get cancelled.

And we know that P’s weight equals U’s.

Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight Persons viz, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are

sitting on a square table and all of them are

faces inside but not necessarily in the same

order. Four of them sit on the corner of the table

and the rest of them sit on the side of the table.

Further, it is known that all persons have either

different or the same weights.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight family members - Alice, Babita, Karan,

Manish, Preeti, Priyanka, Rohit, and Sachin have

gone for a dinner. They are seated around a

square table such that four persons are seated

at the corner and four persons are seated in

the middle of each side. All are facing the

center. They all belong to a family of three

generations. Each member of the family has

ordered a different food viz. Pizza, Noodles,

Burger, Momos, Dosa, Manchurian, Idli, and

Pasta but not necessarily in the same order.

Preeti is seated at one of the corners. Babita is

seated second to the right of Preeti. Only two

persons are seated between the one who has

ordered Momos and the one who has ordered

Manchurian. Priyanka is the mother of Manish,

who is the grandson of Sachin. Alice who is

married to Rohit is seated fourth to the left of

Preeti, who has ordered Dosa. There are two

persons seated between the one who has

ordered Dosa and the one who has ordered

Momos. The one who has ordered Idli is seated

second to the left of Priyanka. Alice is the

mother of sister-in-law of Preeti. Priyanka is

seated immediate left of Preeti. Rohit is the

immediate neighbor of both Alice and Karan.

Babita, who is the mother of Preeti, is married to

Sachin. Manish, who has ordered Momos, is

seated immediate right of Babita. Priyanka has

ordered Noodles. Babita has neither ordered

Pizza nor Burger. Alice has not ordered Burger.

Preeti is the aunt of Manish.

Find the odd one out from the following.

explanation:

Kartik.

2. M likes Kiara and sits to the immediate right

of N, who does not like Kartik.

Case I: When N sits second to the left of the

one who likes Kartik

Case II: When N sits 2nd to the right of the one

who likes Kartik

likes Salman.

who likes Kartik.

likes Deepika sits to the immediate right of the

one who likes Ranveer, which is not possible in

case I so, case I is invalid.

explanation:
D sits third to the right of the one who likes

Carrot. One person sits between D and the one

who likes Orange. The one who likes Carrot sits

third to the left of V. 

The one who sits second to the right of the one

who likes Carrot is facing M. A likes Mango. The

person A is the immediate neighbor of the one

who likes Radish and P. 

The one who likes Orange sits third to the right

of J. Therefore, Case-1 gets eliminated. Not

more than one person is seated between J and

G. The one who likes Turnip sits second to the

right of the one who likes Potato. S does not like

Turnip.

G and one who likes Pear are not immediate

neighbors. The one who likes Turnip and one

who likes Apple are not immediate neighbors.

Therefore, Case-2a gets eliminated. 

This is the final arrangement.

explanation:
Arrangement 1:

1. K sits third to the right of P who sits at the

corner.

2. One person sits between K and R.

3. U sits third to the right of R.

4. One person sits between U and M.

(Here, case 1 will be eliminated and we will

continue with the case 2)

7. Q is not a neighbor of P and F is not a

neighbor of R.

Arrangement 2:

The person sitting at the corners read in class I

and the person sitting at the sides read in class

II.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H were

seated along two concentric square shaped

parks facing towards the centre. 4 of them were

seated along the outer bigger park such that

one person was there along each of the side. 4

of them were along the inner smaller park such

that there was one person at each of the

corner. Each person wore a different coloured

shirt viz. green, white, yellow, blue, pink, brown,

purple and red not necessarily in the same

order. The positions were numbered 1-4 as

shown in the figure given below:

The person who wore the brown shirt and B had

the same seat number, B was not the one with

the brown shirt. G's seat number was 2 more

than B's seat number and they were seated

along the outer park. D's seat number was

twice as that of the seat number of the one

who wore the purple shirt. D was at one of the

corners and was to the immediate left of the

one who wore the white shirt. A was wearing

the red shirt and was seated at one of the

corners. H's seat number and the one who wore

the blue shirt was the same, H was not the one

with the blue shirt. E was to the immediate right

of the one who wore the yellow shirt. F's seat

number was twice as that of the person's seat

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

The following Persons: P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, W, X, Y, Z,

and L are the twelve people seated at the two

concentric square tables. Eight of them are

seated at the outer table facing the center,

while the remaining four are seated on the inner

table's corner facing the outside. Four of them

are seated at the corner of the outer table, and

four more are seated in the center of the side.

The order of the information is not always the

same. One person sits between S and Y. The

one who sits second to the right of Y is facing W

who is facing outside the center. Z chooses to

sit at the outer table rather than next to X or Y.

The person sitting to Z's immediate right is

facing R. The number of
person seated

between Z and X is the same as between Z and

P. The person facing L is seated second to V's

right. The number of people sitting between V

and Q is the same as the number seated

between Q and the person facing T. Q sits at

the outer table and is neither sit adjacent to Z

nor Y. S sits third to the left of X. U is seated

third from the right of the person adjacent to Z.

What is the position of the one who faces T

with respect to X?
